Okay, let's move forward. Now there are three parts to any script. First is the opener introduction. Second is the main body of the script. And then third comes the close. Let's go through each of them one at a time. First, the opener introduction. And your first rule is that it needs to be short. Now let's go with an outbound sales call as an example here. And we'll assume that we're either calling someone on the phone or walking in their door to try to sell them something. Okay, and the first four steps are number one, you introduce yourself. Then step two, you tell them the reason why you're there today. Then you have step three is you ask them if they're interested in what you have to offer because you don't waste their time of yours. And then step four, you ask them for permission to qualify them. Okay, now let me break these four things down for you. Give you a bit more detail on each. And let me give you a few keys here. Number one, you're going to be enthusiastic from the very beginning. Two, you want to always use the familiar. In other words, you wouldn't say, hey, it's Mr. Jones there. You'd say, hi, it's John there. You want to sound familiar. Number three, you introduce yourself and your company very quickly in the first couple of sentences. Okay, number four, you then want to restate the name of your company a second time. So for instance, you might start off and say, you know, hi, John, this is Jordan calling from Sales Security in Melbourne. How's it going today? Then you'd say after a set say, now, John, you probably heard of Sales Security. What are the fastest growing blah, blah, blah, you get it. So you say the name of your company twice within the first couple of sentences. Okay, number five, use powerful words. These words like dramatically explosive, fastest growing, most well respected. Those power words go a long way to capturing someone's attention and establishing your own credibility as an expert. Okay, number six, don't forget to use your justifiers. And again, I went through this in the prospecting module. I know you've all been through that already. It was justifies like so, meaning, you know, a couple of questions. So I don't waste your time or the reason for the call today is a reason why you're calling to those your justifiers. Number seven, you want to ask for permission to begin the qualification process. And I just mentioned this a second ago, a couple of quick questions. So I don't waste your time, right? So is your justifier. Okay, number eight, listen carefully. You cannot qualify effectively if you're not listening. Remember, a qualifications all about. It's about intelligence gathering about identifying your client's needs, their desires, some of their beliefs if possible, and of course, any pain they might have. So that's why you're actually qualifying. And of course, you're also qualifying to make sure they're financially qualified. They have money to afford your product, your good or your service. Okay, so if you're not listening, then all of this speaking to them is worthless. You get a gather intelligence to get information that will help you fill the clients need quickly and elegantly as you move down the straight line. Now, at the end of your introduction, you then have a transition into the main body of your presentation. And it doesn't have to be any more complicated than this. Well, John, based on everything you just said to me, this program is definitely a perfect fit for you. Now, one, you notice the subtle differences in tonality here. First, here's the casual one. Well, based on everything you just said to me, this is definitely a perfect fit for you. That's the first way. Now, here's the second way. Well, John, let me say this, based on everything you just said to me, this is definitely a perfect fit for you. Which one has more power to you? Obviously the second, right? And the operative word here was definitely, and I said it with a hard D. It was like definitely a perfect fit for you. That again is the tone of certainty and also a tone of enthusiasm and there's a tone of confidence attached to it as well, right? So that's your transition to the main body.
